steps.title: Observe that x coefficients are opposites
steps.content:
The two equations are:
$$y = \frac{x}{4} + 6$$
$$y = -\frac{x}{4} + 30$$
The x coefficients are $\frac{1}{4}$ and $-\frac{1}{4}$—exact opposites. Since we need $2y$, adding the equations will eliminate the x terms entirely.

steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate x
steps.content:
Add the left sides and right sides together:
$$y + y = \left(\frac{x}{4} + 6\right) + \left(-\frac{x}{4} + 30\right)$$
Combine like terms on the right:
$$2y = \frac{x}{4} + \left(-\frac{x}{4}\right) + 6 + 30$$
$$2y = 0 + 36$$
$$2y = 36$$
D is correct.
